text:
Once upon a time, in the heart of the dense forest, there was a small village known simply as Willowbrook. Every year, on Decoration Day, the townsfolk made their beloved owl statue come alive.

This particular year, the owl turned a brilliant shade of azure. Its feathers looked just like feathers of moonlit leaves. The villagers decided to call him Orion.

Orion strutted up to the center of town Square. He'd sit quietly on a branch above the headlights, watching children. They would clap their hands and throw berries at him.

As dusk settled, Orion would descend gracefully. Sometimes he’d let out a soft hoot, startling a fewleeps awake. Laughter filled the air.

One rainy afternoon, a young girl named Lily noticed Orion wasn Owl. She loved owls. She’d watch them from afar.

Today was different. Orion seemed listless. Still?

Lily approached slowly. Orion didn’t swoosh away. Maybe he sensed her fear?

Her
